name: Puerto Vallarta
summary: Puerto Vallarta is generally safe for tourists, ranking as one of Mexico's most secure beach destinations with strong police presence in tourist zones. While violent crime is rare, travelers should be aware of petty theft and common tourist scams. Recent cartel activity in February 2026 temporarily disrupted the city but normalcy has returned.
quickVerdict: Puerto Vallarta remains a relatively safe destination for tourists, with crime rates lower than many major U.S. cities. Tourist areas like the Malecon, Zona Romantica, and Marina are well-patrolled and secure. The main risks involve petty theft, overcharging scams, and isolated incidents outside tourist zones. February 2026 saw temporary disruption due to cartel retaliation following El Mencho's death, but the situation has stabilized. With basic precautions and awareness, visitors can safely enjoy this popular Pacific coast destination.

neighborhoods.name: Zona Romantica (Old Town)
neighborhoods.description: The historic heart with cobblestone streets, restaurants, and the main beach. Heavy police presence and tourist patrols make this the safest area for visitors.

neighborhoods.name: Marina Vallarta
neighborhoods.description: Upscale marina district with luxury hotels, golf courses, and yacht clubs. Private security and controlled access points provide excellent safety for tourists.

neighborhoods.name: Hotel Zone Norte
neighborhoods.description: Main hotel strip along the coast with resorts and tourist facilities. Well-monitored by tourism police with good lighting and regular patrols.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Centro
neighborhoods.description: Central business district with markets and local shops. Generally safe during daytime but requires more caution at night and awareness of surroundings.

neighborhoods.name: Pitillal
neighborhoods.description: Local residential area with higher crime rates including theft and burglary. Not a typical tourist destination but requires extra caution if visiting.

neighborhoods.name: Las Juntas/East of Libramento
neighborhoods.description: Outlying areas east of the main highway with higher crime rates and limited police presence. Tourists should avoid these areas, especially at night.

scams.name: Mustard/Sauce Distraction Scam
scams.description: Scammers spill mustard or sauce on you and offer to help clean it while accomplices pickpocket your belongings.
scams.howToAvoid: Be suspicious of anyone who approaches you after something spills on you. Keep valuables secure and walk away from overly helpful strangers.

scams.name: Timeshare Presentations
scams.description: Aggressive salespeople at airports and popular areas offer free transportation or meals in exchange for attending high-pressure timeshare presentations.
scams.howToAvoid: Book transportation in advance and firmly decline any offers for free services. If it sounds too good to be true, it probably is.

scams.name: ATM Skimming
scams.description: Devices attached to ATMs steal card information and PIN numbers for fraudulent use.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks rather than street machines. Cover your PIN entry and check for unusual devices on the card slot.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Unlicensed taxis or drivers without meters charge excessive rates, especially to tourists unfamiliar with local prices.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official yellow taxis with visible licenses, agree on prices beforehand, or use ride-sharing apps like Uber for transparent pricing.

soloFemale.overview: Puerto Vallarta is considered one of Mexico's safest destinations for solo female travelers, with a welcoming atmosphere and strong security presence in tourist areas. Many women travel here alone successfully, though standard precautions apply. The city has a liberal mindset and LGBTQ+ friendly environment that extends to solo female visitors.
tips: Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in tourist zones like Zona Romantica or Marina
Avoid walking alone at night, especially outside main tourist areas - take taxis or go in groups
Never leave drinks unattended and be cautious of drink spiking in bars and clubs
Keep emergency contacts readily available and share your location with trusted friends
Trust your instincts and remove yourself from situations that feel uncomfortable

nightSafety.overview: Nighttime safety in Puerto Vallarta is generally good within tourist zones, which maintain active police patrols and good lighting. The Malecon waterfront and main entertainment areas are considered safe for evening activities. However, caution increases outside tourist areas and when alcohol is involved.
tips: Stay within well-lit tourist areas like Zona Romantica and the Malecon after dark
Use official taxis or rideshare apps rather than walking long distances at night
Avoid excessive alcohol consumption and never accept drinks from strangers
Travel in groups when possible and keep emergency contacts easily accessible
Be extra cautious in less touristy areas and avoid isolated streets or beaches

transport.metro: Puerto Vallarta does not have a metro system. Public buses are the main form of mass transit.
transport.rideshare: Uber operates legally in Puerto Vallarta with good coverage and transparent pricing. Generally safe and reliable, though not available directly at airport terminals - must cross pedestrian bridge for pickup.
transport.taxis: Official yellow taxis with visible licenses are generally safe. Most lack meters so agree on fares beforehand. Airport taxis are more expensive but reliable. Avoid unlicensed vehicles.
transport.tips: Public buses cost about 10 pesos and are safe during daylight hours. For convenience and safety, stick to official transportation options and avoid hitchhiking or unlicensed vehicles.

customs: Greet people individually with handshakes; personal greetings are important in Mexican culture
Use formal titles like Señor/Señora unless invited to use first names
Tipping 10-15% is customary at restaurants and expected for good service
Try basic Spanish phrases - locals appreciate the effort even if your Spanish isn't perfect
Punctuality expectations are relaxed for social events but important for business

health.overview: Puerto Vallarta has good healthcare infrastructure with modern private hospitals like CMQ and San Javier that cater to international patients. Many doctors speak English and provide quality care at lower costs than North America.
health.water: Hotel and restaurant water is generally treated and safe. Bottled water is widely available and recommended for sensitive stomachs. Avoid tap water and ice from unknown sources.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required beyond routine immunizations. Consult travel medicine clinic 4-6 weeks before travel for personalized recommendations.
health.altitude: Puerto Vallarta is at sea level with tropical climate. High humidity and heat require sun protection, hydration, and gradual sun exposure to prevent heat exhaustion.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Consular Agency: Paradise Plaza Mall, Nuevo Vallarta - 322-222-0069

faq.q: Is Puerto Vallarta safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Puerto Vallarta is generally safe for tourists and considered one of Mexico's most secure beach destinations. Tourist areas have strong police presence and crime rates are lower than many U.S. cities, though standard travel precautions apply.

faq.q: Is Puerto Vallarta safe at night?
faq.a: Tourist areas like Zona Romantica and the Malecon are well-patrolled and safe for evening activities. However, avoid walking alone in non-tourist areas after dark and always use official transportation for late-night travel.

faq.q: Is Puerto Vallarta safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, many solo female travelers visit Puerto Vallarta safely. The city has a welcoming atmosphere, but women should take standard precautions like staying in tourist zones, avoiding excessive alcohol, and not walking alone at night.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Puerto Vallarta?
faq.a: Avoid areas east of the Libramento highway, especially Las Juntas and outlying neighborhoods. Also be cautious in Pitillal and avoid isolated beaches or streets. Stick to established tourist zones for the safest experience.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Puerto Vallarta?
faq.a: Yes, buses are safe and affordable during daytime hours. Official yellow taxis are generally safe when you agree on fares beforehand. Uber operates legally and provides transparent pricing with good safety features.
